overall_summary: All 7 validation checks passed. The Compatibility Ontology v1.0.0 is structurally sound, internally consistent, and ready for use as the governing framework for Layer 3 rule production. No duplicate entities, relationships, or rule types were detected. All lifecycle transitions are valid. All relationship source and target types resolve to declared entity types. All rule types contain required validation logic. The ontology is cleared for production use as the governance framework for the Dynamic Compatibility and Configuration Compliance Engine Layer 3.

validation_checks.description: Verifies that no two entity definitions share the same entity_name value within compatibility_entities.json.
validation_checks.details: All 10 entity names are unique. No duplicates detected.

validation_checks.description: Verifies that no two relationship definitions share the same relationship_name value within compatibility_relationships.json.
validation_checks.details: All 20 relationship names are unique. No duplicates detected.
validation_checks.note: SUPPORTED_BY is included as a virtual inverse definition for vocabulary completeness. It is explicitly flagged with materialize_inverse: false and carries a note that it must not be stored as a physical edge. Its presence as a vocabulary entry does not constitute a materialized duplicate.

validation_checks.description: Verifies that no two rule type definitions share the same rule_type value within compatibility_rule_types.json.
validation_checks.details: All 6 rule type names are unique. No duplicates detected.

cross_reference_check.description: All rule_type values in raw candidates (normalized_rule_candidates.json) are covered by the defined rule types.
cross_reference_check.coverage: 100% — all observed raw rule types are defined in the ontology.

validation_checks.description: Verifies that all allowed_next_states values in compatibility_lifecycle.json correspond to valid lifecycle state names, that no allowed transition references an undefined state, that terminal states declare no outbound transitions, and that no circular transitions are defined.

transition_validity_results.note: The deprecated -> approved reinstatement path is valid but requires escalation governance. The existence of this transition does not constitute a cycle because it is constrained by approval requirements.

cycle_analysis.analysis: The state machine contains one reversible path: candidate -> validated -> candidate (validated can return to candidate for rework). This is a valid rework loop, not a governance cycle. The deprecated -> approved reinstatement path is gated by escalation requirements and does not constitute an unconditional cycle. No other cycles exist.
cycle_analysis.terminal_state_compliance: Both terminal states (rejected, archived) declare empty allowed_next_states arrays. archived correctly lists no further transitions.

validation_checks.details: All 7 lifecycle state transition definitions are valid. No undefined state references. No invalid cycles. Terminal states confirmed.

validation_checks.check_name: Relationship Source and Target Type Validity
validation_checks.description: Verifies that all source_types and target_types values in compatibility_relationships.json reference entity names defined in compatibility_entities.json or declared external types (Layer1CanonicalEntity, ApprovalRecord, OntologyValidationReport).

declared_external_types.description: Entities registered in the Layer 1 canonical_entity_registry (DRV-*, FW-*, OS-*, SEC-*, MGT-* namespaces). Explicitly declared as an external cross-layer reference target.
Governance approval record. An administrative entity not defined in the compatibility entity model but referenced as a APPROVED_BY target. Declared as an external administrative type.
The validation report itself. Referenced as a VALIDATED_BY target. Declared as an external administrative type.

per_relationship_results.note: Target type OntologyValidationReport is a declared external administrative type.
Target type ApprovalRecord is a declared external administrative type.
Target type Layer1CanonicalEntity is the declared cross-layer reference target.

validation_checks.details: All 20 relationships reference valid entity types. All 3 external type declarations are documented and justified.

validation_checks.description: Verifies that every rule type definition in compatibility_rule_types.json includes a non-empty validation_logic object containing at minimum: evaluation_model, compliance_pass_condition, compliance_fail_condition, and at least 3 evaluation steps.
validation_checks.details: All 6 rule types have complete validation logic definitions including evaluation model, evaluation steps, pass and fail conditions, and special handling notes.

validation_checks.description: Verifies that the master compatibility_ontology.json correctly declares all component files, and that the entity_count, relationship_count, rule_type_count, and lifecycle_state_count values in the master document match the actual counts in the component files.
validation_checks.details: All 7 consistency checks passed. Master ontology document accurately reflects the content of all component files. No count mismatches, version inconsistencies, or missing file references detected.

readiness_criteria.criterion: All lifecycle transitions reference valid states
All relationships reference valid entity types
All rule types have complete validation logic
Master ontology document is consistent with component files

cleared_for: Use as governance framework for Layer 3 CompatibilityRule production
Future Neo4j import of approved rules
Future Qdrant embedding of approved evidence records
not_cleared_for: Direct Neo4j import of rule instances (no rule instances have been created yet — this is the ontology framework only)
Production compliance evaluation (requires rule instances in approved state)
